# explore — ARCHITECTURE.md
# Last Updated: 2026-06-02
# Status: CURRENT SOURCE OF TRUTH — generated by ARCHITECT 2026-06-02

## Feature Overview

The `explore` feature is the platform-wide search and discovery surface for VCSM. It
provides a full-screen search bar with filter tabs (Citizens, Vports, Vibes, Districts),
dispatching query-driven searches across actors (via identity RPC), posts (via `vc.posts`
direct query), and feature destinations (Wanders injection). When no query is active, a
discovery feed (`ExploreFeed`) is rendered — currently feature-flagged off. The route is
`/explore` (auth-required).

**Source Path:** apps/VCSM/src/features/explore/
**Engine Path:** engines/hydration (via `@hydration` alias — fire-and-forget actor cache warm on search results)

---

## Layer Presence

| Layer | Present | Path |
|---|---|---|
| Controllers | YES | `apps/VCSM/src/features/explore/controller/` |
| DALs | YES | `apps/VCSM/src/features/explore/dal/` |
| Models | YES | `apps/VCSM/src/features/explore/model/` |
| Hooks | YES | `apps/VCSM/src/features/explore/hooks/` |
| Screens | YES | `apps/VCSM/src/features/explore/screens/` |
| Components | YES | `apps/VCSM/src/features/explore/ui/` (11 components + 1 nested feature subdir) |
| Adapters | NO | None — no adapter barrel exists for this feature |
| Use Cases | YES | `apps/VCSM/src/features/explore/usecases/` |
| Engine controllers | NO | None — engine access is a fire-and-forget DAL-level call |
| Engine DALs | YES (indirect) | `engines/hydration/` via `@hydration` alias in controller |

---

## Active Controllers

| Controller | Purpose | Auth Gate |
|---|---|---|
| `searchResults.controller.js` | Orchestrates multi-source search: calls `searchDal`, fires hydration warm, normalizes + dedupes results, injects feature results (Wanders) | None — no auth check at controller level; relies on Supabase RLS and caller session context |
| `searchTabs.controller.js` | Thin dispatcher — calls `searchDal` with full pagination opts (`limit`, `offset`, `viewerActorId`) and returns flat results | None — same pattern |

**Note on auth gates:** Neither controller calls `requireUser()` or a session check
directly. Auth enforcement is fully delegated to:
(a) Supabase's `identity.search_actor_directory` RPC (server-side), and
(b) the `/explore` route being non-public in the router (`public: false` in `ui/index.jsx`).
There is no in-controller auth gate. This is LOW risk for a read-only search surface but
should be noted.

**Dual implementation risk:** A `usecases/search.usecase.js` file exists that duplicates
the orchestration logic of `searchResults.controller.js` (both call `searchDal` and run
`normalizeResult` + `dedupeByKindAndId`). The use case uses `Promise.allSettled` (more
resilient); the controller uses `Promise.all` (fail-fast). The use case is not imported
by any hook — it appears unused. This is dead code.

---

## Active DALs

| DAL | Tables / RPCs | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `search.dal.js` | `identity.rpc('search_actor_directory')`, `vc.from('posts')` | Unified dispatch DAL — routes query to actor, post, or tag searches based on `filter` arg; returns arrays of promises; `videos` and `groups` filter arms return `Promise.resolve([])` stubs |

**Tables accessed:**
- `identity` schema — `search_actor_directory` RPC (params: `p_viewer_domain`, `p_viewer_actor_id`, `p_query`, `p_filter`, `p_limit`, `p_offset`)
- `vc` schema — `posts` table (columns: `id`, `actor_id`, `text`, `title`, `tags`, `created_at`; filtered by `deleted_at IS NULL`, `is_hidden = false`, tag `contains` and text `ilike`)

**Stub arms:** `videos` and `groups` filter cases return empty arrays. No query is issued.
These represent planned but unbuilt search verticals.

---

## Active Hooks

| Hook | Calls | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `useSearchScreenController` | `ctrlSearchResults` | Primary search hook — manages query/debounce/filter state, module-level TTL cache (45s, max 120 entries), inflight deduplication, cancellation safety |
| `useSearchActor` | `useSearchScreenController` (re-export) | Thin alias — re-exports `useSearchScreenController` with no additional logic |
| `useSearchTabsActor` | `ctrlSearchTabs` | Alternative search hook — no module-level cache, uses ref-based request-id cancellation, also handles Wanders feature injection client-side |

**Duplicate injection risk:** Feature result injection (Wanders card) is performed
independently in two places: `searchResults.controller.js` (`buildFeatureResults`) AND
`useSearchTabsActor.js` (inline `wantsWanders` block). Both paths produce the same
`result_type: 'feature'` object. If `useSearchTabsActor` is wired to the screen,
Wanders injection would fire twice. Currently `useSearchTabsActor` is not wired to
`SearchScreen.view.jsx` (the active screen uses `useSearchScreenController`), so no
visible duplicate — but the dual logic is a maintenance hazard.

---

## Engine Dependencies

| Engine | Alias / Import Path | Purpose | Direction |
|---|---|---|---|
| `hydration` | `@hydration` → `engines/hydration/` | Fire-and-forget actor cache warm after search results contain actor IDs | explore → engines/hydration |

The hydration call is `hydrateActorsByIds(actorIds).catch(() => {})` — fully fire-and-forget.
Failure is silently swallowed. No return value is used.

---

## Cross-Feature Dependencies

| Feature | What Is Imported | Import Surface | Boundary Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `onboarding` | `OnboardingCardsView` | `@/features/onboarding/adapters/onboarding.adapter` | COMPLIANT — adapter-gated |
| `shared` | `SkeletonRow` | `@/shared/components/Skeleton` | COMPLIANT — shared primitive |

**No direct internal cross-feature imports detected.** All cross-feature access is via
adapter or shared layer boundaries.

---

## Authorization Pattern

This feature has no in-code authorization gate. Access control is enforced at two boundaries:

1. **Route level:** `ui/index.jsx` exports `public: false` for the `/explore` route — the
   router must reject unauthenticated access before reaching the screen.
2. **Database level:** `identity.search_actor_directory` is an RPC that receives
   `p_viewer_actor_id` — the server can apply visibility rules based on viewer context.
   The `vc.posts` queries filter on `deleted_at IS NULL` and `is_hidden = false` but do
   not scope to a viewer actor — any authenticated session can read any non-hidden post.

**Risk:** If the route-level auth guard is bypassed or misconfigured, there is no fallback
controller-level check. For a read-only search surface on the LOW tier this is acceptable,
but the absence of a `requireUser()` gate in the controller should be documented.

---

## Module Independence Classification

**MOSTLY INDEPENDENT**

Reason: The feature has its own complete DAL, model, controller, and hook layers with no
internal cross-feature imports. Its only external dependency is a fire-and-forget engine
call (`@hydration`) and a single adapter import from `onboarding` in the view layer.
The `usecases/` layer is dead code that shadows the controller.

---

## Architecture State

**EVOLVING**

Reason: Two structural issues create ongoing drift — (1) dead `search.usecase.js` shadows
`searchResults.controller.js` with a more resilient implementation that is never consumed,
and (2) Wanders feature injection is duplicated across the controller and `useSearchTabsActor`.
The discovery feed (`ExploreFeed`) is permanently hidden behind a hardcoded `false` flag
with no feature-flag system wired. Videos and groups search stubs are unbuilt.

---

## Known Structural Risks

1. **Dead use case (`usecases/search.usecase.js`):** Duplicates controller orchestration
   with `Promise.allSettled` (more resilient than controller's `Promise.all`). Never
   imported. Risk: maintenance confusion — contributors may wire it and create a second
   execution path.

2. **Duplicate Wanders injection:** `buildFeatureResults` in `searchResults.controller.js`
   and the inline `wantsWanders` block in `useSearchTabsActor.js` both produce the same
   feature card. If `useSearchTabsActor` is ever wired to the active screen, the card
   will appear twice.

3. **Broken import path in `ui/index.jsx`:** Imports `ExploreScreen` from
   `@/features/explore/screen/ExploreScreen` (singular `screen`) but the actual directory
   is `screens/` (plural). This is a hard module resolution error if `ui/index.jsx` is
   consumed at runtime.

4. **No controller-level auth gate:** Both controllers (`searchResults`, `searchTabs`)
   perform no session check. Auth is fully delegated to route guard and Supabase RLS.
   Acceptable for LOW tier but should be formally noted.

5. **ExploreFeed permanently disabled:** `SHOW_EXPLORE_DISCOVERY_BLOCKS = false` is a
   hardcoded constant with no feature-flag system wired. The discovery feed (Citizens Row,
   Vports Row) never renders. Not a bug but a dead code surface.

6. **`useSearchActor` is a no-op alias:** Re-exports `useSearchScreenController` with
   zero modification. Either should be removed or the alias needs to serve a distinct
   purpose (e.g., actor-only filter pre-set).

7. **`normalizeResult` dual-path duplication in model:** The model has both
   `mapActorSearchResult`/`mapVportSearchResult`/etc. (type-specific pure mappers) and
   `normalizeResult` (generic dispatcher). The RPC normalizer (`normalizeActorRow`) adds
   a third path for actor rows with dual-key output (`actorId` + `actor_id`,
   `displayName` + `display_name`). This dual-field pattern inflates every actor result
   object and signals an incomplete normalization contract.

---

| Area | Status | Evidence | Missing / Risk |

| Owner defined | FAIL | No OWNERSHIP.md; no explicit owner signal | IRONMAN not run |
| Entry points mapped | PASS | `screens/ExploreScreen.jsx` → `ui/SearchScreen.view.jsx`; route at `/explore` | Broken import in `ui/index.jsx` |
| Controllers present | PASS | 2 controllers present | Dead use case shadows controller |
| DAL/repository present | PASS | 1 unified DAL with multi-source dispatch | Videos/groups stubs unbuilt |
| Models/transformers | PARTIAL | Model present but has 3 parallel normalization paths | Dual-field output on actor rows is a technical debt item |
| Hooks/view models | PASS | 3 hooks; primary hook has TTL cache and inflight dedup | `useSearchActor` is a no-op alias |
| Screens/components | PASS | 1 screen, 1 view, 11 UI components | `ExploreFeed` permanently disabled via hardcoded flag |
| Authorization path mapped | PARTIAL | Route guard + Supabase RLS; no controller-level gate | Missing `requireUser()` in controllers |
| Engine dependencies mapped | PASS | `@hydration` fire-and-forget identified | None |
| Tests/validation noted | FAIL | No test files found in feature directory | SPIDER-MAN not run |

---

## Recommended Handoffs

| Command | Reason |
|---|---|
| **VENOM** | No security posture established; no controller auth gate; post search reads all non-hidden posts without viewer scope — needs trust boundary audit |
| **SPIDER-MAN** | Zero test files; primary search hook has caching logic, inflight deduplication, and cancellation safety that warrant regression coverage |
| **IRONMAN** | No ownership file; feature has multiple structural risks that need an assigned owner |
| **HAWKEYE** | Verify `/explore` route guard is active; confirm `ui/index.jsx` broken import is not in the live router bundle |

---

## Final Module Status

**MOSTLY COMPLETE**

The core search path (controller → DAL → model → hook → screen) is fully built and
functional. The feature is blocked from COMPLETE status by: dead use case, duplicate
injection logic, broken import in route barrel, no tests, no auth gate in controllers,
and a permanently disabled discovery feed.
